back_translation: Therefore, since we have such a great number of witnesses surrounding us like a cloud, let us cast off everything that weighs us down, and the sin that easily entangles, and let us run with endurance the contest that is set out before us.

By fixing our eyes on Jesus, the pioneer and the perfecter of the faith, who because of the joy set before Him endured the cross, disregarded the shame, and was enthroned at the right side of the throne of God.
For consider Him who endured such opposition from sinners, so that you would not grow weary and lose heart.
In your struggle against sin, you have not yet had to resist to the point of shedding blood.
And you have forgotten the encouragement-word that addresses you as sons: 'My son, do not despise the discipline of the Lord, and do not lose heart when He rebukes you,
for the Lord disciplines the one whom He loves, and He scourges every son whom He receives.'
Endure suffering as discipline. God is treating you as sons. For what son is there whom his father does not discipline?
But if you remain without the discipline that everyone has shared in, then you are illegitimate children and not true sons.
Furthermore, we used to have fathers of our flesh as those who disciplined us, and we gave them respect; will we not all the more submit to the Father of our spirits, and live?
For on one side, our fathers used to discipline us only for a few days as they considered fitting; but on the other side, God disciplines us for our benefit, so that we may share in His holiness.
Every kind of discipline at the present time seems not to be joy but sorrow; later on, however, it gives a fruit of righteousness and peace to those who have been trained by that discipline.
Therefore strengthen the limp hands and the weak knees of yourselves.
And make straight paths for your feet, so that what is lame will not be dislocated from the joint, but rather will be healed.
Pursue peace with everyone, and holiness, without which no one will see the Lord.
Watch carefully, that no one may fall short of the grace of God; that no root of bitterness may grow up to cause disturbance, and through that root cause many to be defiled.
Let no one be a sexually-immoral person, or a person who despises God like Esau, who sold the rights of being firstborn of his own in exchange for a single meal.
For you already know that afterward, when Esau wanted to inherit the blessing, he was rejected; he could not find a place for repentance, even though he had sought it with tears.
For you have not come to a mountain that can be touched and which is burning with fire, with thick darkness, deep gloom, and a stormy wind,
and the sound of a trumpet, and the voice of words, which those who heard begged urgently that no further word be added to them.
For they could not bear what was commanded: 'If even an animal touches this mountain, it must be stoned.'
And what appeared was so terrifying that even Moses said, 'I am terrified and trembling.'
But you have come to Mount Zion, and to the city of the living God, namely the heavenly Jerusalem, and to myriads of angels in joyful assembly,
and to the church of the firstborn whose names have been registered in heaven, to God who is judge of all people, and to the spirits of the righteous who have been made complete,
and to Jesus the mediator of a new covenant, and to the blood of sprinkling, which speaks better than the blood of Abel.

Watch carefully — do not refuse Him who speaks. For if those people could not escape when they refused Him who warned them on earth, we will all the more not be able to escape, if we turn away from Him who speaks from heaven —
He whose voice in that time made the earth tremble. But now He has promised, saying, 'Once more, I will shake not only the earth, but also the heavens.'
And the words 'Once more' indicate the removal of the things that can be shaken, because they are made things — so that the things that cannot be shaken may remain.
Therefore, since we are receiving an unshakable kingdom, let us have thankfulness, by which we will serve God in a way that is pleasing to Him, with reverence and awe;
for 'our God is a consuming fire.'
